message:
security(crypto): Harden AES-GCM encryption with strict length validation

Add defensive length validation to prevent subtle cryptographic misuse:

Encryption layer improvements (src/encryption.rs):
- Define AES_GCM_KEY_LEN (32), AES_GCM_NONCE_LEN (12), AES_GCM_TAG_LEN (16) as constants
- Add validate_length() helper with descriptive error messages
- Validate key length (must be exactly 32 bytes for AES-256)
- Validate nonce length (must be exactly 12 bytes for GCM)
- Validate tag length on decryption (must be exactly 16 bytes)
- Add ciphertext length check before tag extraction to prevent underflow
- Replace magic numbers with named constants throughout

Security test updates (tests/security_nonce.rs):
- Add tests for length validation edge cases
- Verify proper rejection of invalid key/nonce/tag lengths
- Test error message clarity for debugging

These changes prevent potential vulnerabilities from:
- Truncated keys (would silently use partial key material)
- Invalid nonce sizes (could compromise GCM security guarantees)
- Malformed ciphertext (could cause panics or undefined behavior)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
